calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Advanced From Oct-19-16 To Oct-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Continued To Oct-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susanne Cohen for (administrator) Marsha Grandchamp.

The Court stated that this is a petition for preliminary distribution, that it seeks to distribute 75% and asked counsel about the 50% limit. Ms. Cohen cited 11620 and that it allows for all or a portion of distribution. The Court stated that it does not say that and that 11623 sets limits. Ms. Cohen said that she reviewed the statutes based on the examiners notes, that she checked again this morning and that 11620 allows for distribution of all or a portion, that 11623 says that the personal representative can use other provisions, that 11621 states that the Court can order distribution if it can be made without a loss to creditors or beneficiaries, that they had hoped to file a petition for final distribution but are unable to do so as there is one asset which may or may not be a part of the estate, that it is a partnership in a ranch in Montana, that documents indicate that Ms. Grandchamp becomes a limited partner after Mr. Winter, that the general partner has had a stroke and is unable to help with the matter, that Ms. Grandchamp has hired counsel in Montana, that they have not been able to get information on the value and are still working on it. The Court stated that it will cost money and may lead to litigation. Ms. Cohen stated that she does not think there will be litigation, that they have hired an appraiser and have permission to view the records and that it is a 5% interest in the ranch. The Court continued the Petition for Preliminary Distribution to October 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to submit legal authority, and that it the Court can grant it at that time it will. The supplemental brief/points and authorities are to be filed by October 19, 2016 and the Court encouraged counsel to review 11620 & 11600.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-05-2016 Continued To Oct-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Continued To Oct-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susanne Cohen for (Petitioner) Marsha Grandchamp.

The Court asked Ms. Cohen if she would like to address the presumption of revocation. Ms. Cohen stated that she filed a declaration with the petition, that she has nothing further to provide, that they opened probate to transfer 2 bank accounts totaling $178,500.00, that the petitioner is named as beneficiary on all other assets and that the property in the 2 accounts is community property. The Court assigned Michael Kosalas as probate referee and granted the petition for letters of administration with (lost) will annexed. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
